title: ‘Feisty I think is the word’: The Boston vs. New York rivalry arrives in NWSL for Legacy FC’s debut
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC played their NWSL debut against Gotham FC, attracting over 30,000 fans to Gillette Stadium.
Gotham FC defeated Legacy FC 1-0 with Esther González scoring the only goal, as the match saw eight yellow cards.
Legacy FC coach Filipa Patão emphasized her team's aggressive playstyle, despite a player being sent off with a red card.

title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys kicked off their St. Patrick’s Day weekend shows at MGM Music Hall at Fenway on March 13.
The band played a mix of hits including 'Barroom Hero' and politically charged songs like 'Citizen I.C.E.'.
Supporting acts included The Vigilantes, Haywire, and Showcase Showdown, energizing the crowd before the headliners.

title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, is running the Boston Marathon to support the Joslin Diabetes Center and those with Type 1 diabetes.
Bennett, from New Braintree, Mass., was diagnosed with Type 1 diabetes at age 11, impacting his sports and social life.
The Joslin Diabetes Center provided Bennett with tools and confidence, motivating him to advocate for a diabetes cure.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter receives her fifth Oscar nomination, becoming the most-nominated Black woman in Oscar history.
In 2019, Carter became the first Black person to win an Oscar for Best Costume Design for 'Black Panther.'
Carter is designing costumes for a Netflix project starring Denzel Washington as Hannibal Barca.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools face over 200 layoffs if voters reject a proposed property-tax override on May 5 election.
Officials highlight need for $18.5 million over three years to maintain staffing and programs in Brookline schools.
Possible cuts include music and language courses, increased class sizes, and new fees for after-school programs.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The PETS Act aims to ban the sale of dogs and cats in Massachusetts pet shops.
The bill proposes partnerships with shelters for adoptions and addresses housing and insurance pet ownership barriers.
The Massachusetts Legislature is considering the PETS Act, which needs legislative approval and governor's signature.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts officials elevate Central and Northeast regions to Level 3 Critical Drought despite February snowfall.
State's Drought Management Task Force reports limited snowmelt and continued precipitation deficit strain water resources.
Residents urged to conserve water as spring rains needed to restore groundwater and surface water levels.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Chanté Joseph's essay on Instagram trends explores why some women hide relationships online.
Joseph's work questions traditional heterosexual norms and suggests a shift in modern relationship dynamics.
Her essay prompted widespread discussion, inspiring a song and podcast exploration by The Boston Globe.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O'Neill, 27, runs the Boston Marathon for The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp supporting children with medical conditions.
The camp offers a safe environment allowing children to enjoy fun activities and support at no cost.
O'Neill's fiancée's sister, a camp beneficiary during her illness, now participates as a counselor in remission.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Eagles have decided not to trade A.J. Brown to the Patriots at this time, per reports.
Trade talks between the Patriots and Eagles for A.J. Brown may resume closer to June 1 for cap relief.
The Patriots reportedly offered first- and third-round picks for Brown, but the Eagles denied the proposal.
